title: Claude reviews an ABAP pull request with SAP context
subtitle: Claude/GitHub replay based on the ABAP CI/CD sample: activated source, syntax, ABAP Unit, ATC, and review evidence.
outcome: A PR review that cites live SAP evidence instead of only reading the Git diff.

start.text: Review PR #20 in the ABAP CI/CD sample. The diff touches ZCL_ARC1_TEST_HELPERS and ZCL_ARC1_TASK_SERVICE. Use ARC-1 read-only context from SAP before commenting.

panel.title: GitHub pull request
panel.body: Changed files: src/zcl_arc1_test_helpers.clas.abap, src/zcl_arc1_task_service.clas.testclasses.abap. Local diff adds a helper for cancelled tasks and extends the ABAP Unit fixture. GitHub has the diff; ARC-1 checks what the SAP system has activated.

read-service.summary: ARC-1 reads the activated method from SAP so Claude can compare the PR against runtime truth.
read-service.result: METHOD list_tasks. SELECT * FROM zarc1_t_task WHERE status IN @status_range INTO TABLE @rt_tasks. SORT rt_tasks BY priority due_date. ENDMETHOD.

panel.title: Activated SAP source
panel.code:
METHOD list_tasks.
  SELECT * FROM zarc1_t_task
    WHERE status IN @status_range
    INTO TABLE @rt_tasks.
  SORT rt_tasks BY priority due_date.
ENDMETHOD.

read-domain.summary: The review checks the domain values the helper is supposed to normalize.
read-domain.result: Fixed values: A = Active, D = Done, X = Cancelled. Data element ZARC1_E_STATUS uses this domain.

syntax.summary: The review runs the SAP syntax check, not just abaplint.

panel.title: SAP syntax check
panel.body: The changed helper class is syntactically valid in the target SAP system.

unit.summary: ARC-1 runs ABAP Unit in SAP for the changed class.

atc.summary: ATC is included as a deterministic SAP-side gate before the AI posts a review.
atc.result: {"variant":"DEFAULT","findings":[{"priority":"P3","object":"ZCL_ARC1_TEST_HELPERS","message":"Test helper has no production usage. Informational only."}]}

panel.title: ATC review signal
panel.body: The PR is not blocked. Claude can mention the P3 warning as low severity and avoid inventing a higher-risk issue.

decision.prompt: How should Claude finish the PR review?

options.label: Post review
options.description: Generate the evidence-backed pull request review.

options.label: Check dumps
options.description: Show the operations follow-up path before posting.

review.text:
## PR #20 review — **no blocking issues**

Checked against what SAP actually has activated, not just the diff:

- ✅ Read activated `ZCL_ARC1_TASK_SERVICE->list_tasks`
- ✅ Confirmed `ZARC1_D_STATUS` allows `X = Cancelled`
- ✅ SAP syntax check on `ZCL_ARC1_TEST_HELPERS` — clean
- ✅ ABAP Unit — **7/7 passing**
- ⚠️ ATC — one **P3 informational** finding, on the test helper only

**Suggested human check:** keep the new cancelled-task fixture readable, and note in the PR description why `X` is now covered.

dump.summary: The same GitHub workflow pattern can attach ST22 evidence to a PR or issue.

done.text: That's the GitHub pattern from the sample repo: abapGit and abaplint own the file workflow, while ARC-1 adds activated source, SAP syntax, ABAP Unit, ATC, and ST22 evidence the diff alone can't show.
